followups.original_pain_point: Website lacks SSL certificate + not mobile-friendly
followups.subject: One more thing I spotted for Blossom Beauty Studio — Sarah
followups.body:
Sarah,

Following up on my note from last week about the SSL and mobile issues on the Blossom Beauty Studio site.

I had another look — blossombeautystudio.co.uk isn't showing up in the top results for 'beauty salon York' on Google Maps. The SSL issue is part of the reason Google deprioritises it, but a few Google Business Profile fields also aren't fully optimised — the services section, categories, and photo library all have gaps that affect local ranking.

For a beauty studio in York, being visible when someone searches for a treatment is worth more than any paid ad. Happy to run a free check and show you exactly what's missing.

[Your name]
followups.angle_rationale: Shift from technical deficit (SSL/mobile) to commercial discovery (local search visibility). Beauty studios in York compete on local search prominence — 'beauty salon York' and 'beauty treatment York' are high-commercial-intent queries. This connects the existing technical issue (SSL is a ranking factor) to the commercial outcome they care about.
followups.notes: DUE TODAY. New angle: local SEO ranking and Google Business Profile gaps rather than SSL/mobile technical fix. Allow 30-min gap after Day-3 bumps before starting Day-7 batch. Check IMAP first — if Sarah replied to initial email or Day-3 bump, do NOT send.

followups.subject: Something else I spotted for Meadowbrook Vets — Dr Rowe
followups.body:
Dr Rowe,

I sent a note last week about the social media presence for Meadowbrook Vets — your 142 Google reviews are a genuinely strong asset that most practices would envy.

I had a look at how the practice appears when people search for a vet in Skipton. Meadowbrook isn't in the top three results for 'vet Skipton' on Google Maps, which means anyone searching for an appointment — or in a pet emergency — may not find you first. The practice website also doesn't surface your Google reviews or qualifications prominently, so the trust signals you've built over the years aren't doing the work they could be on first contact.

Happy to put together a short report on both. Just reply and I'll send it over.

[Your name]
followups.angle_rationale: Shift from social media absence (reviews not amplified on social channels) to local search visibility and website trust signals. Vets compete on emergency searches and proximity — 'vet Skipton', 'vet near me', 'emergency vet North Yorkshire' are all high-intent queries. Dr Rowe's 142 reviews are the credibility anchor; the angle is that those reviews aren't converting into new patients because local search ranking and website trust signals are weak.
followups.notes: DUE TODAY. Use 'Dr Rowe' throughout — formal address appropriate for a veterinary practice principal. New angle: local search ranking and website trust signals (shift from social media amplification). Check IMAP first. Stagger 20 mins after fu_033.

followups.subject: One more thing I spotted for Thornfield Electricals — Mike
followups.body:
Mike,

Following up on my note from last week about the SSL issue on thornfieldelectricals.co.uk.

I had another look — the site isn't showing up in the top results for 'electrician Bradford' on Google Maps. The missing SSL is part of the reason Google deprioritises it, but the Google Business Profile listing is also missing the correct trade category, has no recent photos, and shows no customer reviews response — these are the signals Google uses to decide which local electricians to show first.

The SSL fix I mentioned is quick, but sorting the Business Profile takes about 20 minutes and often makes an immediate difference to ranking. Happy to run a free check and send you what I find.

[Your name]
followups.angle_rationale: Shift from technical trust deficit (SSL flagged unsafe) to commercial discovery gap (invisible in local search). 'Electrician Bradford' is a high-commercial-intent query with direct revenue impact. Frames the SSL fix as one piece of a broader ranking solution — lowers the perceived scope of the ask while increasing the commercial relevance. Same pattern as fu_017 (Halifax Window Cleaning) which worked well.
followups.notes: DUE TODAY. Short and commercial — trades audience. New angle: Google Maps local ranking gap (shift from SSL safety warning). Stagger 20 mins after fu_034. Check IMAP first.
